reasons: Angela helps Freya stay alive after Freya commits with her jump or ultimate.
Freya's extended-fight style benefits from extra shield and support while she continues basic attacks and skill cycles.

proof.summary: Freya dives and keeps fighting, while Angela adds protection that helps Freya remain active inside the fight.
worksBestWhen: Angela saves Heartguard for Freya's real engage.
Freya commits when follow-up damage or control is available.
failureCases: Freya commits before Angela can attach.

reasons: Mathilda gives Freya extra reach and repositioning support.
Freya can start fights without spending all of her own mobility immediately.

proof.summary: Mathilda improves Freya's entry and chase path, while Freya supplies the sustained melee damage.
worksBestWhen: Mathilda uses her mobility support before Freya's commit or during chase.
Freya follows the route into a target she can keep attacking.
failureCases: Freya overextends past Mathilda's support range.
Enemies disengage before Freya reaches melee range.

reasons: Minotaur can add healing and layered crowd control around Freya's melee engage.
Freya benefits when enemies are held in place long enough for her sustained attacks to matter.

proof.summary: Minotaur stabilizes the teamfight with healing and knock-up pressure, while Freya continues melee damage inside the controlled area.
worksBestWhen: Minotaur enters before or alongside Freya.
Freya is close enough to continue attacking during the control window.
failureCases: Freya is forced to disengage before using her damage window.

reasons: Tigreal can group enemies and create a clear melee entry for Freya.
Freya can follow the grouped crowd control with sustained damage and Valkyrie pressure.

proof.summary: Tigreal starts the teamfight by pulling or stunning enemies, then Freya enters to continue the fight at close range.
worksBestWhen: Freya is close enough to enter immediately after Tigreal's setup.
Tigreal engages when Freya's ultimate or damage window is ready.
failureCases: Tigreal engages too far away from Freya.
Freya is controlled before reaching the grouped enemies.

reasons: Kaja can lock one priority target and drag them into Freya's melee range.
Freya can continue the fight with basic attacks and skill pressure after the suppression window.

proof.summary: Kaja isolates a target, and Freya follows with sustained melee pressure.
worksBestWhen: Kaja pulls a squishy or mobile target toward Freya.
Freya has enough resources to continue attacking after the drag.
failureCases: The target survives the first window and Freya is kited afterward.
Kaja catches a low-value frontline target.
